---
title: "Descriptive Statistics"
subtitle: "Summarise your data with means, standard deviations, and grouped summaries."
toc: false
---

<details class="topic-card" open>
<summary>Setup</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Run the setup chunk first â€” it creates the dataset used in all examples below.

```{webr-r}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

capsules <- tibble(
  capsule_id   = 1:20,
  batch        = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 10),
  fill_mass_mg = c(174.2, 174.4, 174.7, 174.0, 174.5,
                   174.3, 174.6, 174.1, 174.8, 174.4,
                   175.1, 174.8, 175.3, 174.6, 175.0,
                   175.2, 174.9, 175.4, 175.0, 175.3),
  pct_label    = c(99.8, 100.1, 100.3, 99.6, 100.0,
                   100.2, 99.9,  100.4, 99.7, 100.1,
                   100.5, 100.2, 100.7, 99.9, 100.4,
                   101.0, 100.6, 100.9, 100.3, 100.8)
)

capsules
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>Basic statistics</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Single-value summaries of a numeric vector. Always print with `cat()`.

```{webr-r}
cat("Mean % label claim:", signif(mean(capsules$pct_label), 5), "%\n")
cat("SD:                ", signif(sd(capsules$pct_label),   3), "%\n")
cat("Median:            ", signif(median(capsules$pct_label), 5), "%\n")
cat("Min / Max:         ", min(capsules$pct_label), "/", max(capsules$pct_label), "%\n")
cat("n:                 ", nrow(capsules), "capsules\n")
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>summarise( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Compute multiple statistics at once and return them as a tibble.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    mean_pct  = mean(pct_label),
    sd_pct    = sd(pct_label),
    min_pct   = min(pct_label),
    max_pct   = max(pct_label)
  )
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>group_by( ) + summarise( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Repeat the summary for each level of a grouping variable.

```{webr-r}
capsules %>%
  group_by(batch) %>%
  summarise(
    n        = n(),
    mean_pct = mean(pct_label),
    sd_pct   = sd(pct_label),
    min_pct  = min(pct_label),
    max_pct  = max(pct_label)
  )
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>count( )</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Count the number of rows in each group â€” shorthand for `group_by() %>% summarise(n = n())`.

```{webr-r}
# How many capsules per batch?
capsules %>%
  count(batch)
```

</div>
</details>

<details class="topic-card">
<summary>IQR and quantiles</summary>
<div class="topic-card-body">

Useful for identifying spread and outliers in assay data.

```{webr-r}
cat("IQR:        ", IQR(capsules$pct_label), "%\n")
cat("25th pctile:", quantile(capsules$pct_label, 0.25), "%\n")
cat("75th pctile:", quantile(capsules$pct_label, 0.75), "%\n")
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Coding convention â€” store then print
Compute statistics at full precision, then round only inside `cat()` when reporting.
Never round inside `summarise()` if the values will be used in further calculations.
:::

</div>
</details>
